releaseNotes:
Infra 0.4.0 — July 2026

This release changes what an Infra instance sends out, so those items come first.

- The update check now sends two things and nothing else: the version you are running, and a random installation ID your instance generated for itself. No company name, no hostname, no user or record data. The ID exists so real installations can be counted and supported; it means nothing outside the publisher's registry, and your instance shows it to you.
- Bug reports you escalate carry a DIFFERENT, independently generated ID. Neither is derived from the other, so "this installation is still running" cannot be matched against "this installation reported a bug". Both IDs are visible in your own settings, and linking them is something only you can choose to do.
- Every outbound request is now written to your own log before it is sent. A new page — Settings → Outbound requests — lists every request Infra has ever made, with the full URL as sent and whether it succeeded. If the log entry cannot be written, the request is not sent.
- Update checks moved from daily to weekly, and now run for any signed-in user rather than only a SuperAdmin, so an instance with few admins is not mistaken for an abandoned one.
- Update checks now go to the publisher's own registry rather than to GitHub. The file published is the same one.
- New: send a bug report to the publisher, with a review screen first. A SuperAdmin can escalate one specific report. Before anything leaves, the screen shows the exact payload — including the screenshot, which can be removed with one click, and your instance's record counts, which can be withheld. Nothing is ever sent automatically, in bulk, or in the background, and a report is sent once.
- Turn the update check off at any time by clearing the update URL in Admin Settings or unsetting VERSION_CHECK_URL. You then stop being told when a fix ships; nothing else changes.

history.releaseNotes:
Infra 0.3.0 — July 2026

- Easier self-hosting: Infra now ships as a prebuilt container image with a complete step-by-step setup guide — download, set two values, and run.
- Navigation & UX polish: breadcrumbs on every detail and form page, a reorganized Settings hub, and a single unified "Add" action for linked records.
- Automatic updates: instances now record their version automatically on deploy (no manual step), and admins see an update banner with release notes and a download link.
- Security & platform: upgraded the underlying application framework and cleared all high-severity dependency advisories.
- Cloud storage: added Azure Blob Storage as a durable file-storage option for cloud and multi-host deployments.
